architecture_summary: The paper proposes a unified Vision-Language Pre-training (VLP) model that uses a shared multi-layer Transformer network for both encoding and decoding, and is pre-trained with bidirectional and sequence-to-sequence masked vision-language prediction objectives controlled by self-attention masks.

novel_contributions: a unified Vision-Language Pre-training (VLP) model that can be fine-tuned for both vision-language generation and understanding tasks
a shared multi-layer Transformer network for both encoding and decoding
two unsupervised vision-language prediction tasks: bidirectional and sequence-to-sequence (seq2seq) masked vision-language prediction
the first reported model that achieves state-of-the-art results on both vision-language generation and understanding tasks across COCO Captions, Flickr30k Captions, and VQA 2.0

key_claims.claim: VLP is a unified model that can be fine-tuned for both vision-language generation tasks such as image captioning and understanding tasks such as VQA.
The model uses a shared multi-layer Transformer network for both encoding and decoding, unlike methods that use separate encoder and decoder models.
VLP is pre-trained on large amounts of image-text pairs with bidirectional and seq2seq masked vision-language prediction objectives.

The paper explicitly claims VLP is the first reported model to achieve state-of-the-art results on both vision-language generation and understanding tasks across COCO Captions, Flickr30k Captions, and VQA 2.0.
On COCO Captions test set with CIDEr optimization, Unified VLP achieves 39.5 B@4, 29.3 M, 129.3 C, and 23.2 S.
The unified model significantly speeds up downstream learning and improves accuracy compared to random initialization or language-only pre-training.
The paper reports that seq2seq pre-training helps captioning tasks, while bidirectional pre-training benefits VQA.
